News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Prospects For New Round Of US-Iran Talks Clouded Two Days Before Truce Expires RFE/RL 20 Apr 2026 -- The prospects for a new round of face-to-face peace talks between the United States and Iran remained unclear amid confusion over the US negotiators' plans and uncertainty over whether Tehran would agree to take part.

  • US Navy deploys unmanned robots to clear sea mines in Strait of Hormuz: Report Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 20 Apr 2026 -- In the aftermath of a temporary halt in hostility, United states Navy has deployed sea drones and other robotic systems to help clear mines laid at the bottom of the Strait of Hormuz, according to FirstPost. All this is being done to restore the passage of commercial shipping through this vital waterway.
  • Trump says US forces intercepted Iranian cargo ship breaching naval blockade Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 20 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said Sunday that American naval forces intercepted and disabled an Iranian-flagged cargo ship that attempted to breach a US naval blockade in the Gulf of Oman, with Marines now holding the vessel in custody.
  • US Fires On, Seizes Iranian Ship, While Fate Of Cease-Fire, Peace Talks Uncertain RFE/RL 20 Apr 2026 -- US forces have fired at and seized an Iranian-flagged ship in the Gulf of Oman that was attempting to evade a US maritime blockade, President Donald Trump and the Pentagon said on April 19, while Iran's military command angrily threatened to retaliate.

  • Iran forces U.S. troops to retreat from Strait of Hormuz, opening new shipping route People's Daily 20 Apr 2026 -- The Navy of Iran's Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) reportedly forced U.S. troops to retreat on Sunday after they opened fire on an Iranian commercial ship near the Gulf of Oman, the semi-official Mehr news agency reported.
  • Iranian Military Command Vows Revenge For US Seizure Of Cargo Ship RFE/RL 20 Apr 2026 -- Iran's top joint military command accused US forces of violating a cease-fire by shooting at and seizing an Iranian commercial vessel in the Gulf of Oman and vowed to retaliate "soon," according to state media.
  • Iran says to respond to "armed maritime piracy" by U.S. -- media People's Daily 20 Apr 2026 -- Iran's top joint military command said on Monday that Iran will soon respond to "armed maritime piracy" by the U.S. military, according to media reports.
  • Iran's Hormuz iron fist shatters Western 'vibe economy': Qalibaf exposes US Treasury house of cards Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Speaker of the Iranian Parliament Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf has laid bare the fragile, sentiment-driven foundations of Western financial systems amid escalating tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, in an insightful commentary that underscores the Islamic Republic's strategic leverage over global energy markets.

  • Trump Says Deal US Is Seeking With Iran 'Far Better' Than JCPOA RFE/RL 20 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump is asserting that a deal the United States is negotiating with Iran will be much better than the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA), the 2015 international agreement on Iran's nuclear program from which he withdrew the United States during his first term.
  • Trump Says Vance, US Delegation To Arrive In Pakistan 'Tonight' For Iran Talks RFE/RL 20 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump told the New York Post that a delegation led by Vice President JD Vance was due to arrive in Pakistan "tonight," but there was no immediate confirmation that Vance had left the United States and Tehran has not said it will take part in a new round of peace talks.

  • Iran to attend 2nd round of talks with US despite Strait of Hormuz hostilities Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 20 Apr 2026 -- Iran will take part in the second round of talks with the US in Islamabad despite the latest hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz, two Pakistani sources familiar with the mediation process told Anadolu Agency on Monday.
  • Qalibaf: No talks under threat, Iran prepared to reveal new cards on the battlefield Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf has said that Tehran will not accept negotiations with Washington under the shadow of threats.
  • April 20: Iran rejects pressure as US maritime terrorism casts ominous cloud over diplomacy Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- The next round of talks between Tehran and Washington remains shrouded in uncertainty following renewed American aggression at sea, after the US Navy violated the ceasefire by targeting an Iranian merchant vessel in the Sea of Oman.
  • Iran doesn't trust US in talks, will respond decisively to any threats: IRGC Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Commander of the Aerospace Force of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says Iran does not trust negotiations with the United States aimed at ending the illegal American-Zionist aggression but puts faith in national power.
  • US seeks Iran's surrender; Iranians don't submit to force: Pezeshkian Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian says Iranians will never submit to force despite "bitter messages" from American officials.
  • US has no option but to drop excessive demands, respect Iran's rights: Deputy FM Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh says the United States has no option but to abandon its excessive demands and fully respect the rights of the Iranian people, adding that diplomacy is the only solution to the crisis.
  • Iran's military vows 'decisive action' against US army after attack on Iranian vessel Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Iran's military has vowed to take decisive action against the "terrorist US Army," following an American attack on an Iranian commercial vessel.
  • Iran: US not serious about talks, will face decisive response if it repeats past mistakes Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- The Iranian Foreign Ministry says the United States is not serious about negotiations with Iran, asserting that Washington will face the Islamic Republic's decisive retaliation if it repeats its past mistakes concerning the country.
  • 'War benefits no one': Pezeshkian urges unity among Muslim nations to foil foreign plots Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian says strengthening unity among Islamic countries is the most important way to counter conspiracies and prevent foreign powers from taking advantage.
  • Araghchi says US conduct shows 'bad faith', lack of seriousness in diplomacy Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the United States pursues malicious goals and lacks the necessary seriousness in its engagement with the Islamic Republic.
  • No 'free security' in Strait of Hormuz if Iranian oil exports are restricted: VP Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Vice President Mohammad Reza Aref says there will be no "free security" for others in the Strait of Hormuz if Iranian oil exports are restricted.
  • Real Talks Need Honored Pledges, Not US Pressure - Iranian President Sputnik 20 Apr 2026 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian stressed that honoring commitments is essential for dialogue, while condemning U.S. demands for Iran's surrender.
  • Iran Slams US Attack on Merchant Ship as Act of Aggression Sputnik 20 Apr 2026 -- Iran's joint military command pledged that Tehran would respond soon and called the US attack against an Iranian-flagged container ship near the Strait of Hormuz an act of piracy that violated the ceasefire that has been in place since April 8.
  • Iranian Armed Forces Vow Swift Response to US Seizure of Iranian Vessel in Gulf of Oman Sputnik 20 Apr 2026 -- A spokesman for Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya Military Command said that Iran would respond swiftly to the seizure of an Iranian vessel by the US military in the Gulf of Oman.
  • Iran rejects taking part in 2nd round of peace talks with U.S. -- official news agency People's Daily 20 Apr 2026 -- Iran's official news agency IRNA said on Sunday the country has rejected taking part in the second round of the peace talks with the United States.

  • China's Xi Urges Reopening Of Hormuz RFE/RL 20 Apr 2026 -- In the most high-profile statement from Beijing on the US blockade of Iranian ports so far, Chinese leader Xi Jinping told Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman on April 20 that "the Strait of Hormuz should remain open to traffic."
  • China urges parties to abide by ceasefire, avoid exacerbating tensions to create conditions for resumption of navigation: FM spokesperson on US interception of ship sailing toward Iran Global Times 20 Apr 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Monday that the situation in the Strait of Hormuz is sensitive and complex. "We express concern over the forced interception of relevant vessel by the US." Guo's remarks came after a media inquiry that US intercepted a cargo ship sailing toward Iran, which Iran vowed retaliation and called it a "piracy" act, and that Tehran said it will not participate in a second round of talks.
  • Xi says normal passage through Strait of Hormuz should be maintained PRC MND 20 Apr 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping on Monday said normal passage through the Strait of Hormuz should be maintained.
  • China voices concern over US attack on Iranian merchant vessel Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- China has voiced its concern over the US attack on an Iranian vessel in the Sea of Oman, warning against any provocations.

  • Russia calls US naval blockade of Iran unlawful, urges continued talks Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov has called the United States' naval blockade of Iran and its recent seizure of an Iranian cargo ship unlawful, while calling for continued talks between Iran and the US to avoid a return to military confrontation.
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's telephone conversation with Foreign Minister of Iran Abbas Araghchi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 Apr 2026
  • Rosatom to Restart Work at Iranian Bushehr Nuclear Plant Once Peace Returns - CEO Sputnik 20 Apr 2026 -- Russian state nuclear corporation Rosatom plans to resume construction of new units at the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant in Iran as quickly as possible once peace is restored, the company's CEO Alexey Likhachev said on Monday.

  • Pakistan's army chief tells Trump blockade of Iran hampering efforts to resume talks Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Pakistan has told US President Donald Trump that the continued US naval blockade of Iranian ports is hampering Islamabad's efforts to mediate a second round of negotiations between Iran and the US aimed at permanently ending the US-Israeli aggression against Iran.

  • President pledges 'practical contribution' to reopening Hormuz KOREA.net 20 Apr 2026 -- President Lee Jae Myung on April 17 pledged to "substantially contribute to guaranteeing the freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz" in an international videoconference aimed at reopening the strategic waterway.
  • UAE slams Trump for Iran war fallout, warns of shift to yuan amid dollar shortages: Report Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- The United Arab Emirates (UAE) has blamed US President Donald Trump for the economic fallout from the US-Israeli war on Iran, warning that it will conduct financial transactions in yuan if the Federal Reserve does not establish a currency swap line with the Emirati central bank.
  • President Xi Jinping Speaks with Saudi Arabia's Crown Prince and Prime Minister Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud on the Phone PRC MofA 20 Apr 2026 -- On the afternoon of April 20, 2026, President Xi Jinping took a phone call from Saudi Arabia's Crown Prince and Prime Minister Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud.

  • Wary Of Hormuz Instability, Eurasian Logistics Look To Middle Corridor RFE/RL 20 Apr 2026 -- While diplomatic efforts struggle to stabilize access to the Strait of Hormuz amid tensions between the United States and Iran, Eurasian trade is increasingly being redirected toward overland alternatives, with the Trans-Caspian Transport Route, also known as the Middle Corridor, emerging as a key diversification route in Eurasian logistics.

  • Internet Shutdown Adds To The Costs Of War For Iranians RFE/RL 20 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Internet shutdown has now surpassed 1,248 hours or 52 days, making it the longest national-level blackout since Libya's outage of nearly six months during the Arab Spring in 2011.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Presidential Determination Concerning the Air Force's Jet Fighter Training Operations in Idaho, Oregon, and Nevada The White House 20 Apr 2026
  • Fact Sheet: President Donald J. Trump Issues Presidential Determination to Support U.S. Air Force Jet Fighter Training Operations The White House 20 Apr 2026
  • Reauthorization of Small Business Innovation Research and Technology Transfer Programs DoD 20 Apr 2026 -- Following President Donald J. Trump signing into law the Small Business Innovation and Economic Security Act (S. 3971), the War Department is immediately advancing with a redesigned and more focused initiative to accelerate the delivery of advanced capabilities to the warfighter. The act reauthorizes the vital Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs, strengthening America's lethal Arsenal of Freedom.
  • Department Official Details DOW's Intelligence, Security Priorities DoD News 20 Apr 2026 -- The undersecretary of war for intelligence and security testified on the resourcing, priorities and challenges facing the intelligence enterprise for fiscal year 2027, during a hearing before the House Armed Services Committee's intelligence and special operations subcommittee in Washington, April 16.
  • US EUCOM Encourages Vigilance Mindset EUCOM 20 Apr 2026 -- As part of its ongoing commitment to the safety of its personnel, U.S. European Command reminds all U.S. service members, civilian employees, contractors, and their families about the enduring importance of force protection and cybersecurity. Consistent, proactive personal security measures are fundamental to protecting the whole community and ensuring mission readiness.
  • 919th SOW tests full-spectrum readiness in wing exercise US Air Force 20 Apr 2026 -- For the first time in more than 10 years, the 919th Special Operations Wing validated its full mission cycle in a comprehensive combat readiness exercise held from April 8-12. The event proved the wing's ability to rapidly transition from a home-station force to a deployed combat unit.
  • Coast Guard's sole heavy icebreaker returns home following Antarctic deployment US Coast Guard 20 Apr 2026 -- The crew aboard the U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Polar Star (WAGB 10) returned home to Seattle on April 13, following a 146-day Antarctic deployment in support of Operation Deep Freeze (ODF) 2026.
  • Chile Welcomes Nimitz Carrier Strike Group US Navy 20 Apr 2026 -- Ships of the Nimitz Carrier Strike Group (NIMCSG), including Nimitz-class aircraft carrier USS Nimitz (CVN 68) and Arleigh Burke-class guided missile destroyer USS Gridley (DDG 101), arrived in Valparaiso, Chile, for a scheduled port visit on April 17, 2026, as part of U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command/U.S. 4th Fleet's Southern Seas 2026 deployment.
  • Australia, Canada, and U.S. conduct multilateral operations in South China Sea US Navy 20 Apr 2026 -- Ships from the Royal Australian Navy, Royal Canadian Armed Forces, and U.S. Navy conducted multilateral operations in support of a free and open Indo-Pacific in the South China Sea, April 12-18.
  • 45th Weather Squadron Enables Joint Recovery Efforts in Human Spaceflight United States Space Force 20 Apr 2026 -- From launch to splashdown, human spaceflight is often viewed through the lens of rockets and spacecraft. Less visible, but equally critical, is the joint network of organizations working behind the scenes to ensure astronauts return safely to Earth. At the center of that effort, the 45th Weather Squadron, assigned to Space Launch Delta 45, delivers mission-critical weather intelligence that connects military, civilian, and interagency partners across the globe.
  • USSF terminates contract for the Global Positioning System Next Generation Operational Control System United States Space Force 20 Apr 2026 -- The Global Positioning System Next Generation Operational Control System program was cancelled by the Defense Acquisition Executive on April 17, based upon the recommendation of the acting service acquisition executive.
  • CNO Remarks at Sea Air Space US Navy 20 Apr 2026

  • Statement by the North Atlantic Council concerning the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons on the occasion of the 11th Review Conference NATO 20 Apr 2026
  • NSPA hosts second Fuel Industry Day in Luxembourg NATO Support and Procurement Agency (NSPA) 20 Apr 2026 -- On 8 April 2026, the NATO Support and Procurement Agency (NSPA) alongside Supreme Headquarters Allied Powers Europe (SHAPE) and Allied Command Transformation (ACT) held the second Fuel Industry Day at NSPA headquarters in Luxembourg, welcoming more than 180 participants and 65 companies.

  • Threats in Eurasia Increasing Significantly, Require Additional Efforts From CSTO - Lavrov Sputnik 20 Apr 2026 -- The threats in Eurasia are increasing significantly and this requires additional efforts from the Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO), Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said on Monday.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's statement and answers to media questions at a meeting of the Council of the CSTO Parliamentary Assembly, Moscow, April 20, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 Apr 2026

  • Germany Tells Russia Threats Are 'Unacceptable' After Arms Makers List Published RFE/RL 20 Apr 2026 -- Germany became the second country to condemn Moscow over the publication of a list of companies Russia claimed are helping produce attack drones for Ukraine, warning that "direct threats" are "unacceptable."
  • Combat experience and technological adaptability: Ukraine's contribution to shaping new defense standards Ukraine MOD 20 Apr 2026 -- The Ministry of Defence outlines the significance of the rapid integration of domestic defense innovations into NATO standards in developing a resilient European security architecture.
  • Joint Defense Production and Long-Term Security Cooperation: a Meeting with the Danish Delegation Took Place at the Office of the President President of Ukraine 20 Apr 2026

  • Chinese FM releases national report of China's implementation of NPT; policy has injected stability and certainty to the world: FM Global Times 20 Apr 2026 -- The Chinese Foreign Ministry released on Monday the national report of China on the implementation of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT). The ministry's spokesperson said on the same day that China's nuclear policy has injected stability and certainty into the world.
  • China will participate in NPT review conference in a constructive manner, says FM spokesperson Global Times 20 Apr 2026 -- China on Friday submitted its national implementation report to the 11th Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT). This is China's latest voluntary transparency measure aimed at enhancing mutual trust and creating a sound atmosphere for the review conference, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said at a regular press conference on Monday in response to a related question.

  • Defence joins partners for Exercise Balikatan in the Philippines Australia DOD 20 Apr 2026 -- Around 400 members of the ADF are taking part in the 41st iteration of the largest bilateral military exercise conducted between the Philippines and the United States.
  • Australia, Canada and the United States transit through South China Sea Australia DOD 20 Apr 2026 -- Ships from the Royal Australian Navy, Royal Canadian Navy, and United States Navy have conducted a routine multilateral transit through the South China Sea in accordance with international law.
  • Australia, the Philippines, and the United States conduct maritime cooperative activity Australia DOD 20 Apr 2026 -- Australia, the Philippines and the United States recently conducted a Maritime Cooperative Activity (MCA) within the Philippines' Exclusive Economic Zone.
  • Australia and Japan sign contracts for $7bn warships deal Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 20 Apr 2026 -- Australia and Japan have signed contracts for the first three of 11 warships set to be delivered to the Australian navy under a landmark $7bn defence deal, as the two close US allies in the Asia Pacific region deepen defence cooperation, according to Al Jazeera.
  • Engineers fire fruits of their labour Australia DOD 20 Apr 2026 -- Ahead of the first firing of Australian-made long-range strike missiles, Australian engineers at the Port Wakefield manufacturing facility have been learning critical skills of the missile manufacturing trade.

  • DPRK Missile Administration Test-fires Tactical Ballistic Missiles KCNA 20 Apr 2026 -- The Missile Administration of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea conducted a test-fire on April 19 to evaluate the power of warhead of the improved surface-to-surface tactical ballistic missile Hwasongpho-11 Ra.

  • Canadian Armed Forces reach highest recruitment in 30 years Canada 20 Apr 2026 -- Today, Minister of National Defence, David McGuinty, announced that for a second consecutive year, the Canadian Armed Forces surpassed its Regular Force recruiting target. In fiscal year 2025/26, the CAF enrolled 7,310 new members, surpassing its target of 6,957 new members and marking the highest number of enrolments in more than 3 decades.
  • Canadian Armed Forces reach highest recruitment in 30 years Canada 20 Apr 2026

  • France's New Nuclear Strategy to Weaken Security in Europe - Russian Foreign Ministry Sputnik 20 Apr 2026 -- The security of non-nuclear European countries will ultimately be weakened by France's plans to deploy nuclear weapons on their territory, Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Alexander Grushko said in an interview with Sputnik.

  • Poland and France Seal Historic Partnership in Gdańsk Republic of Poland 20 Apr 2026 -- The first Polish-French intergovernmental summit took place in Gdańsk, co-chaired by Prime Minister Donald Tusk and President Emmanuel Macron.

  • LAUNCH OF DSC A 23 (YARD 328) - FOURTH SHIP OF DIVING SUPPORT CRAFT (DSC) PROJECT India PIB 20 Apr 2026 -- DSC A 23, the fourth ship of five Diving Support Craft (DSC) project was launched on 19 Apr 2026 at Titagarh, Kolkata (WB) by Mrs Deepa Sivakumar, in the presence of Vice Admiral B Sivakumar, Chief of Materiel.

  • The Minister of Defence of the Republic of Armenia received Kevin Hamilton, Special Representative of the NATO Secretary General Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 20 Apr 2026 -- On April 20, the Minister of Defence of the Republic of Armenia Suren Papikyan received Kevin Hamilton, Special Representative of the NATO Secretary General for the Caucasus and Central Asia.

  • 'Balikatan' drills not aimed at China, says US military exec Philippine News Agency 20 Apr 2026 -- The "Balikatan" exercises among the Philippines, United States and its allies are not aimed against China, a ranking American military official said on Monday.
  • BrahMos missile system to undergo 'simulation firing' in 'Balikatan' Philippine News Agency 20 Apr 2026 -- The Indian-made BrahMos cruise missile system, considered the Philippines' most powerful weapon in its arsenal, will be subjected to simulation firing during the maritime strike phase of the "Balikatan" military exercises.
  • AFP chief says 'Balikatan' to build more efficient defense systems Philippine News Agency 20 Apr 2026 -- The annual "Balikatan" exercises among the Philippines, United States and its allies are aimed at creating defensive systems that can move and respond efficiently, Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) chief Gen. Romeo Brawner Jr. said on Monday.
  • Blindly binding each other's security will only backfire, bring trouble upon themselves, FM says on US-Philippine military drills as Japan joins Global Times 20 Apr 2026 -- When asked about China's position on the US-Philippine annual joint military exercises launched in the Philippines on April 20, in which approximately 10,000 US military personnel are scheduled to take part, with the Japan Self-Defense Forces also set to participate, and which takes place amid tense China-Philippines and China-Japan relations, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Monday that unilateralism and military bullying have inflicted profound disasters on the world. What the Asia-Pacific region needs most is peace and tranquility, and what it needs least is external forces being introduced to stoke division and confrontation.

  • US Lawmakers Move To Target Chinese, Russian Influence In Georgia RFE/RL 20 Apr 2026 -- As the geopolitical map of the Middle East and Eurasia undergoes a sometimes violent reshuffling, Washington is sharpening its focus on a critical "missing piece" of the democratic puzzle: the Republic of Georgia.

  • Thomas De Waal: Iran War Could Complicate Trump's Caucasus Corridor Plan RFE/RL 20 Apr 2026 -- Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinian recently announced that the Trump Route for International Peace and Prosperity (TRIPP) had entered its implementation phase.

  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Malagasy Prime Minister Rajaonarison US Dept. of State 20 Apr 2026

  • Israel, Lebanon to hold second round of talks in Washington on Thursday Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 20 Apr 2026 -- Israeli and Lebanese representatives will hold talks in Washington on Thursday, a U.S. State Department spokesperson and an Israeli source speaking on the condition of anonymity told Reuters on Monday.

  • Israel flattening civilian buildings in southern Lebanon despite ceasefire Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Israel has continued to systematically flatten civilian buildings in southern Lebanon during the ceasefire, according to an Israeli media report.

  • Hezbollah's roadside bombs pose growing danger to Israeli occupation forces: Israeli media Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- An Israeli paper has reported that the danger posed by roadside bombs in southern Lebanon has resurfaced, becoming a recurring nightmare for Israeli occupation forces.
  • 1,500 face displacement as Israel demolishes Palestinian homes in Naqab Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Israeli forces have resumed months-long "ethnic cleansing" campaign by demolishing Palestinian homes in a Bedouin village in the Negev desert in the southern part of the occupied territories.

  • Security Council Press Statement on Death of French Peacekeeper from United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon UN Security Council 20 Apr 2026 -- The following Security Council press statement was issued today by Council President Jamal Fares Alrowaiei (Bahrain):
  • Gaza: Human development set back 77 years as recovery costs rise to $71 billion UN News 20 Apr 2026 -- The UN and European Union issued a joint warning on Monday that human development across Gaza has been set back by a staggering 77 years, with $71.4 billion needed over the next decade for recovery and reconstruction.
  • UN experts call for immediate suspension of EU-Israel trade agreement as 'minimum requirement' under international law OHCHR 20 Apr 2026 -- The European Union (EU) must immediately suspend the EU-Israel Association Agreement, which has granted Israel preferential access to the European market since 2000, despite well-documented human rights violations that have escalated into atrocity crimes, including genocide, UN experts* said today.
  • UN chief strongly condemns attack killing French peacekeeper in S. Lebanon People's Daily 20 Apr 2026 -- UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres on Saturday strongly condemned the attack that killed one French peacekeeper serving with the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) and injured three others earlier in the day, his spokesperson said in a statement.

  • Ad Hoc Liaison Committee: press remarks by High Representative/Vice-President Kaja KALLAS, Espen Barth Eide, Norwegian Minister for Foreign Affairs and Mohammad Mustafa, Palestinian Prime Minister European External Action Service (EEAS) 20 Apr 2026
  • Norway provides NOK 100 million in budget support to Palestine Government of Norway 20 Apr 2026 -- "The economic situation in Palestine is so precarious that hospitals, schools and other public services are at risk of collapse. We must do what we can to prevent such a collapse. That is why we are now providing Palestine with NOK 100 million in budget support," said Minister of Foreign Affairs Espen Barth Eide.
  • Sweden increases humanitarian support to Lebanon Government Offices of Sweden 20 Apr 2026 -- Sweden is announcing an additional humanitarian support package to Lebanon of SEK 70 million.
  • The OIC Participates in the Ninth Meeting of the International Coalition for a Two-State Solution in Brussels Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 20 Apr 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) participated in the ninth meeting of the International Coalition for the Implementation of the Two-State Solution, which was held in Brussels, Belgium, on Monday, with the participation of representatives of more than 80 countries and international organisations

  • New Movement by China Concerning Unilateral Development of Natural Resources in the East China Sea Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 20 Apr 2026

  • The OIC Welcomes the Adoption of a Unified Budget for the State of Libya Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 20 Apr 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has welcomed the signing of a unified budget for the State of Libya, the first Libyan national budget in over a decade, representing a positive step towards economic and developmental stability for the country as a whole and for the benefit of the Libyan people

  • HRH the Crown Prince, Chairman of Sudan Transitional Sovereignty Council Discuss Latest Developments in Sudan SPA 20 Apr 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, met today in Jeddah with Chairman of Sudan's Transitional Sovereignty Council Lieutenant General Abdel Fattah Al-Burhan and his accompanying delegation

News Reports

  • Announcing Steps to Impose Visa Restrictions on Family Members or Close Personal or Business Associates of Sinaloa Cartel Affiliates US Dept. of State 20 Apr 2026 -- President Trump is using the full power of the United States to eradicate narco-terrorist cartels operating in our hemisphere.

  • Fifth Meeting of the Countering Transnational Terrorism Forum US Dept. of State 20 Apr 2026 -- To target ongoing malign and destabilizing activities by Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS) and their proxies and partners, the U.S. Departments of State and Justice convened the fifth meeting of the Countering Transnational Terrorism Forum (CTTF) at the International Law Enforcement Academy (ILEA) in Budapest, Hungary, on April 14-15, 2026.

  • NASA Welcomes Latvia as Newest Artemis Accords Signatory NASA 20 Apr 2026 -- The Republic of Latvia signed the Artemis Accords Monday during a ceremony hosted by NASA at the agency's headquarters in Washington, becoming the 62nd nation to commit to responsible space exploration for all humanity.
  • United States Welcomes Latvia's Signing of the Artemis Accords US Dept. of State 20 Apr 2026 -- The Department of State congratulates Latvia on joining the Artemis Accords. Latvia is the 62nd country to sign the Accords to date, pledging its commitment to peaceful exploration and use of space.
  • NASA Rolls Out Artemis III Moon Rocket Core Stage NASA 20 Apr 2026 -- Following the recent successful test flight of NASA's Artemis II mission around the Moon, NASA rolled out the core stage, or the largest section, of the agency's SLS (Space Launch System) rocket that will launch the crewed Artemis III mission in 2027. The stage departed from the agency's Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans on Monday for shipment to NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida, marking key progress on the path to the agency's first crewed lunar landing mission to the Moon under the Artemis program in two years.

  • Meeting with Penza Region Governor Oleg Melnichenko President of Russia 20 Apr 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a working meeting with Governor of the Penza Region Oleg Melnichenko.
  • Press release on the summoning of the Mexican Ambassador to the Russian Foreign Ministry Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 Apr 2026
  • Western Policy Aimed at Deterring Russia, China Threatens International Peace - Moscow Sputnik 20 Apr 2026 -- The policy of the West, which is aimed at deterring Russia and China, poses a threat to international peace and security, Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Evgeniy Ivanov said on Monday.
  • Repression in Russia: UK and others express concern at Russian court's decision to classify Memorial as an "extremist organization": Joint Statement to the OSCE UK FCDO 20 Apr 2026 -- UK and others condemn systematic repression of independent voices and civil society in the Russian Federation.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on April 20, 2026 PRC MofA 20 Apr 2026

  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 20 Apr 2026

  • Two spies convicted of working for Israel's Mossad executed in Iran Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Two men convicted of spying for the Israeli Mossad spy agency were executed in Iran after the Supreme Court upheld their sentences following the completion of all legal procedures.

  • Mossad, IDF and ISA Joint Announcement Israel - Prime Minister 20 Apr 2026 -- Mossad, IDF and ISA Joint Announcement: The Fight to Thwart Terrorism Continues - Exposure of a Secret Iranian Terrorist Network behind an Assassination and Terrorism Campaign Targeting Senior Israeli Officials and Strategic Objectives in Israel and around the World
  • PM Netanyahu's Yad Lebanim Remarks for Remembrance Day for the Fallen of Israel's Wars Israel - Prime Minister 20 Apr 2026
  • FM Sa'ar speaks with the President of the European People's Party Manfred Weber Israel MFA 20 Apr 2026 -- FM Sa'ar: "The world's most dangerous regime must not be allowed to obtain a nuclear weapon."

  • Belarus: UN experts condemn Government move to invalidate passports of exiled opponents OHCHR 20 Apr 2026 -- The UN Group of Independent Experts on the Situation of Human Rights in Belarus strongly condemns the Government's decision to invalidate the passports of persons formerly detained on political grounds who were forcibly expelled from the country following their release in 2025 and 2026.
  • Belarus: UN experts warn of torture, suicide attempts, deaths and reprisals in custody OHCHR 20 Apr 2026 -- UN experts* today expressed grave concern over allegations of prolonged incommunicado detention and detention conditions at a Navapolatsk Correctional Colony in Belarus.

  • Meeting Confirmed Between Cuba and US Officials Cuba Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 Apr 2026 -- "Alejandro García del Toro, general deputy director for U.S. Affairs at Cuba's Foreign Ministry (MINREX), confirmed recent foreign press reports on meetings between US and Cuba's officials in Havana."

  • Transcript of Special Briefing by MEA on the Visit of Hon'ble Vice President of India to Sri Lanka India MEA 20 Apr 2026
  • Transcript of Special Briefing by MEA on the State Visit of President of the Republic of Korea to India (April 20, 2026) India MEA 20 Apr 2026

  • Prime Minister Carney speaks with Prime Minister of Luxembourg Luc Frieden Prime Minister of Canada 20 Apr 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, spoke with the Prime Minister of Luxembourg, Luc Frieden.
  • Prime Minister Carney speaks with Prime Minister-elect of Hungary Péter Magyar Prime Minister of Canada 20 Apr 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, spoke with the Prime Minister-elect of Hungary, Péter Magyar.
  • Canada's PM: Ties with US have turned into 'weaknesses,' must be corrected Press TV 20 Apr 2026 -- Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says the economic and political ties with the US have turned into "weaknesses" and must be corrected.

  • Joint Declaration french-polish on the occasion of the First french-polish Bilateral Summit under the Treaty of Nancy. France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 20 Apr 2026
  • The Daily Press Briefing (04/20/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 20 Apr 2026

  • Chinese FM urges Japan to stop flexing muscles, reflect on its history of aggression over Japanese SDF vessel participating in US-Philippine joint drills following Taiwan Straits passage Global Times 20 Apr 2026 -- China has stated its solemn position regarding Japanese Self-Defense Forces vessel passing through the Taiwan Straits, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun remarked on Monday when asked to comment on question regarding the reports that Japan was set to participate in the US-Philippines joint military drills and last week, a Japanese destroyer transited the Taiwan Straits en route to the exercise area.
  • China urges Japan to learn from history, honor its international obligations: Chinese FM spokesperson responds to Japanese protest against Takaichi's constitutional revision push Global Times 20 Apr 2026 -- Responding to a media inquiry about reports that around 36,000 people gathered near the Japanese parliament on Sunday to protest the government of Sanae Takaichi over its push to revise the constitution, holding signs that read "No War! Don't Change the Constitution!" and "Takaichi steps down," while calling for the protection of Japan's pacifist constitution, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said at a regular press briefing on Monday, "We have also noted that Japan's moves toward constitutional revision have triggered growing doubts and opposition within Japan, among neighboring Asian countries and in the international community."
  • The Tenth Japan-UK Foreign Ministers' Strategic Dialogue Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 20 Apr 2026

  • Albares discusses with Commissioner McGrath need to strengthen Europe in light of current geopolitical landscape Spain MFA 20 Apr 2026 -- The Minister for Foreign Affairs, European Union and Cooperation, José Manuel Albares, welcomed the Commissioner for Democracy, Justice and the Rule of Law, Michael McGrath, to Madrid on Monday, with whom he discussed the current geopolitical landscape and the need to defend and strengthen the European Union.

  • World News in Brief: AI diagnostics, humanitarian deal for DR Congo, rights abuse allegations in Belarus, Ukraine children bear heaviest burden UN News 20 Apr 2026 -- New data shows that nearly three in four countries in Europe now use Artificial Intelligence in their health services to make a diagnosis.
